title: azorella fragosea
intro: Azorella fragosea, also known as Paraguay tea or Chuindé, is a plant native to the Andes mountains in South America, which belongs to the Carrot or Bok Choy family. Azorella fragosea has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Azorella fragosea can be consumed as a tea, either as a standalone beverage or combined with other herbs, and has also been used in traditional medicine to prepare infusions and tinctures.
